meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Project Management to Solve Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Master project management techniques using Antigravity and Claude Code to build faster, smarter, and error-free applications.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't about staring at screens; it's about systematic project management. By breaking your build into modular, verifiable chunks and using AI-assisted analysis tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you isolate failures, track logic flows, and resolve complex errors before they compound into technical debt.
content.intro:
The screen is glowing, the error message is cryptic, and you've been stuck on a single line of code for three hours. This is the 'beginner's wall'—the brutal reality where your enthusiasm meets the cold, hard logic of a broken build. It feels like you're lost in a labyrinth, and the more you guess, the further you drift from a functional application.

But here is the secret the pros won't tell you: debugging is not a coding skill; it is a project management skill. When you treat your codebase like a professional sprint rather than a chaotic experiment, you gain the clarity required to kill bugs instantly. We are moving beyond guesswork and into a structured, AI-powered workflow that ensures your project stays on track and your sanity remains intact.

steps.title: Define Modular Scope
steps.description: Before coding, break your features into 'atomic' tasks. If a module takes more than 30 minutes, it's too big to debug efficiently.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current feature scope. Break this into atomic, testable modules that minimize side effects and isolation complexity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude task 'Break my feature requirements into modular, isolated sub-tasks for development.'

steps.title: Establish Baseline State
steps.description: Create a known working state before adding new logic. This prevents 'ghost bugs' from hidden dependencies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit my current codebase state. Identify the minimum viable logic required to establish a stable checkpoint before I add new features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a stable snapshot of the current working directory and verify baseline tests pass.'

steps.title: Implement Traceable Logging
steps.description: Inject structured logs at every transition point. You cannot fix what you cannot observe.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest a logging strategy for this function that exposes state changes without polluting the console.
steps.claude_code_command: claude modify 'Add structured logging to all asynchronous function entry and exit points.'

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Vector
steps.description: Use binary search debugging: comment out half your code to see if the error persists. If it's gone, the bug is in the ignored half.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Identify the specific line or dependency causing this stack trace. Suggest an isolation strategy to isolate the logic fault.
steps.claude_code_command: claude debug --trace 'Run the current test suite and isolate the exact function call causing the exception.'

steps.title: Validate with Unit Tests
steps.description: Never fix a bug without writing a test to prove it's dead. This prevents regressions in your portfolio projects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a unit test for this module that covers the edge case causing my current runtime error.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Generate and run a unit test suite for the newly refactored module to ensure error regression is prevented.'

pro_tips: Treat your error logs as data, not insults; use them to map the 'path of least resistance' for your logic.
Adopt 'Rubber Duck Debugging' with Claude—explaining your code line-by-line to the AI forces you to spot your own logic gaps.
Always commit your code before asking an AI for help; it allows you to roll back instantly if the AI suggests a destructive path.
Ignore 'perfect' code until the MVP works; focus on functional, readable logic first, then refactor for performance later.
Keep a 'Bug Journal'—documenting every error and its solution accelerates your learning curve by 10x compared to just fixing it.
content.next_steps: After mastering this workflow, start building a small-scale project like a personal task manager or a weather dashboard to apply these management techniques in a low-stakes environment.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer a student struggling with errors; you are an engineer managing a system. Apply these steps to your next build and watch your development velocity explode. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
